description_long: This object is a vintage cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a boxy, rectangular form and a woodgrain-patterned exterior casing. The front features a large, convex glass screen bordered by a metallic silver bezel, with a control panel on the right side that includes two large rotary dials and two toggle switches, all set into a wood-textured panel. The sides and top are finished in a faux wood veneer, while the back is a matte black, slightly angled plastic housing that accommodates the bulky CRT components. The television sits on a flat base, with no visible feet or stand, and the overall design is reminiscent of mid-to-late 20th-century home electronics. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the exterior. The object appears solid and heavy, typical of older television sets, and is likely constructed from a combination of wood, plastic, glass, and metal.
description: A vintage CRT television with a woodgrain exterior, convex glass screen, and a side control panel featuring rotary dials and toggle switches.
description_view_0: Side view showing the woodgrain panel and the depth of the CRT housing, with no controls or screen visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the convex glass screen, metallic bezel, and the right-side control panel with dials and switches.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, highlighting the woodgrain panel and the protruding back of the television.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the matte black, angled plastic housing with no visible ports or controls.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick metallic silver bezel with a rectangular shape, surrounding the convex glass screen, and a wood-textured control panel on the right.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large rotary dials and two toggle switches arranged vertically on the right front panel.
